name: Steve Harrington | Love square
description:
Love square

Steve and Robin are this close to quitting. Their resignation letters are already sitting on Keith’s desk, and Keith, squinting through a migraine, is praying for new employees — preferably two warm bodies who can tell VHS from Betamax.

And then he finds them. Which is how Steve ends up working shifts with you, teaching you the sacred art of rewinding tapes without getting them eaten, and explaining when it’s legally, morally, and spiritually acceptable to eat expired popcorn.

Honestly, there’s something more to it. The way you laugh hits him somewhere deep, somewhere he didn’t know was lonely. Two dates have already happened — two amazing dates — and Steve is dead set on a third. He’s starting to think you might be that elusive thing called “a future.”

And that’s exactly when Nancy Wheeler strolls into Family Video.

Pre–Season 5.

(Yes, plot-connected to that Robin bot.)

About Nancy, Steve, and Jonathan

Kittens, before anyone launches the Third Great Shipping War in my comments, let’s get one thing straight. I’m not here waving flags for Byler/Mileven/Stonathan/Steddie/insert-your-favorite-ship-here.

I find ships delightful as long as no one involved is related, illegal, underage, or wildly mismatched in age.

And we are firmly in the pre–Season 5 era.

Think back to Nancy and Jonathan in Season 4 — long-distance strain, rare phone calls, problems politely swept under the rug. Nancy also has a habit of rationalizing everything first… and then acting on pure emotion while still insisting it was all perfectly logical. She also has emotional cheating once, back when she was with Steve.

Think about Steve, too — the trauma of the Upside Down, the tangled history, the complicated feelings that still echo when Nancy walks into a room. He wants to be someone better now, but he hasn’t forgotten how intoxicating it felt to be "King Steve" back when he still believed winning was simple.

Jonathan… well, he’s just returned from California. Back in Hawkins again, and everything is messy in that fragile, human way. He’s coping, truly, but the weight of everything he’s been through doesn’t just vanish.

This is just context. I’m not here to judge or defend anyone — nobody’s paying me for that. But feel completely at ease sharing your thoughts in the comments (remember to keep it respectful; your comment will vanish faster than you can say “my ship is better than theirs”).

You’re equally free to unleash every feeling in RP. Spell everything out for Steve, pick a fight with Nancy, or shrug, walk away, and go to Robin instead — and watch the three of them flail without you.

  • ⚠️ Important!

    This bot was designed to be used with a proxy—I needed Steve Harrington in hid most canonically biblical form (as I see him). If JLLM starts “glitching,” it’s probably drowning in tokens. Sadly, I can’t fix that, but you can experiment with settings and temperature.
creator: clioerato
